raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: memory-bound
REASONING: The kernel performs many global memory loads and stores with irregular, wrapped indexing like `[(i * 17) & 4095]` and `[(i + j * 257) & 4095]`, which tends to defeat coalescing and makes memory access the dominant cost. The arithmetic work is very small—just a handful of multiply-add operations per thread—so there is not enough computation to hide the cost of these scattered memory accesses.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel performs many global memory loads and stores with irregular, wrapped indexing like `[(i * 17) & 4095]` and `[(i + j * 257) & 4095]`, which tends to defeat coalescing and makes memory access the dominant cost. The arithmetic work is very small—just a handful of multiply-add operations per thread—so there is not enough computation to hide the cost of these scattered memory accesses.

Follow the user's requested output format exactly.

Valid bottleneck labels: memory-bound, compute-bound, latency-bound,
occupancy-limited, register-spill
prompt_rendered.user:
Diagnose the primary performance bottleneck 
in this CUDA kernel:

```cuda
__global__ void kernel(float* x, const float* y) { int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x; float v = y[(i * 17) & 4095]; for (int j = 0; j < 8; ++j) { v = v * 1.1f + x[(i + j * 257) & 4095]; } x[i] = v; }
```

What is the primary performance bottleneck? Use exactly this format:
BOTTLENECK: <label>
REASONING: <2-3 sentences>
